rationale: Both candidates converge on the same diagnosis (PR #4834's level-2 wake guard is intentional, test used the wrong sleep level) and both overreach by confidently declaring the issue already fixed on main and recommending closure — the actual thread shows no confirmed resolution, only an attribution to #4834 and a request to trigger a full CI run, so neither answer's closure verdict is thread-verified. Y is somewhat stronger on grounding and completeness: it cites more specific code locations (sleep() setting the flag, the generate() admission guard, three distinct regression tests) and adds practical level-1-vs-level-2 usage guidance, giving a more end-to-end picture, while X is comparatively terser and less detailed. Neither is clearly correct about the resolution status, so correctness is capped similarly for both.
